what_it_does: Records an image attachment entry for the design-assets channel, linking to a specific image asset URI and noting when it was added.
observations: The file consists entirely of YAML frontmatter with no body content
It declares an id (att-420a372e), channel (design-assets), attachment_type (images), target image URI, and an ISO-8601 added timestamp
There are no code symbols present
standouts: References a concrete image asset by URI (image://design-assets/diagrams/Visual Assets - Dev Space - Frame 6)
Serves as a minimal, timestamped ledger record rather than a document
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Fields follow a flat key/value attachment schema: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added
Keeps the asset itself external, storing only a pointer and provenance metadata
notes: This file is a bare attachment ledger stub that records an image reference posted in the design-assets channel.
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an attachment metadata record, which matches the content exactly.
intention_signals: To create a durable, retrievable record of an image attachment shared in the design-assets channel, separating the asset payload from its provenance metadata.
contribution: Provides a lightweight, timestamped metadata entry that captures an attachment's identity, channel, type, target URI, and creation time, supporting retrieval and provenance without storing the asset itself.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML metadata attachment record for an image in the design-assets channel, identifying its target URI and addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To log an image attachment shared in the design-assets channel for later reference and retrieval.
